signals.headline: RLUSD Stablecoin Full Rollout Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
RLUSD Stablecoin Full Rollout Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**
Ripple USD (RLUSD) has completed its official full-scale rollout across the XRP Ledger and Ethereum, driving a massive influx of capital into the ecosystem.[1]

Other active areas include News Stories (102 markets) and Pop Culture, but no X hype[4].

signals.headline: Central Bank Integration Accelerating | YES | MEDIUM confidence**
signals.raw:
Central Bank Integration Accelerating | YES | MEDIUM confidence**
Central banks are increasingly integrating Ripple's payment rails for cross-border settlements as part of the broader institutional adoption wave.[1]

The most significant market-moving confirmation is the SEC resolution combined with RLUSD's full deployment—both represent removal of regulatory uncertainty and expansion of XRP's use

signals.headline: SEC Legal Battle Officially Resolved | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
SEC Legal Battle Officially Resolved | YES | HIGH confidence**
The long-standing SEC legal battle with Ripple is now "officially in the rearview mirror," with XRP ETF approvals entering the final stage.[1]

signals.headline: XRP Breached $1
signals.raw:
XRP Breached $1.50 Resistance for First Time in 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**
XRP touched $1.51094 on April 17, marking its first break above $1.50 since February's selloff.[3] This was driven by institutional spot ETF inflows ($224 million in the first week of April) rather than retail futures trading, suggesting more stable demand.[3]
